description: Jarvis single-threaded the entire Rogers-O'Brien prep workflow — research, content writing, PDF generation, and reMarkable upload — instead of dispatching to agents in parallel. Harper should have built the prep sheet, Knox should have pushed to reMarkable. PDF generation via improving-pdf is shared infrastructure any agent can call. Jarvis doing agent work blocks multi-threading and defeats the purpose of the agent system.
correction: Three rules established: (1) improving-pdf is shared infrastructure — any agent producing a deliverable calls it. (2) Jarvis coordinates, agents execute — if an agent owns the work, route it; prefer parallel dispatch. (3) When Jarvis does work directly because no agent owns it, flag it for Quinn's weekly review as a candidate for agent assignment or capability build.
systemic_fix: Jarvis must dispatch to agents in parallel whenever possible. Single-threading agent-owned work through Jarvis is a routing error. When no agent owns a task, Jarvis executes but logs it as a gap for Quinn to review.
